---
title: "Setting a boot menu option"
date: 2011-10-15
forum: Installation &amp; Upgrades
---

### Post by pwabrahams on 2011-10-15
How can I add "acpi=off" to the boot options for Kubuntu 11.10 (or any other xubuntu)?   Supposedly that's done by editing the appropriate menuentry in **/boot/grub/grub.cfg**, but that file says very clearly at the top that it isn't supposed to be edited:```

# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE
#
# It is automatically generated by grub-mkconfig using templates
# from /etc/grub.d and settings from /etc/default/grub

```
But I can't find any way to do it either in **/etc/grub.d** or in **/etc/default/grub**.  In fact a grep on /etc shows no useful menuentry lines.

This probably isn't the right section for this query, but I can't find a more appropriate one.

---

### Post by oldfred on 2011-10-15
See this, you can add it to standard & recovery or like quiet splash, just to standard boot:

gksudo gedit /etc/default/grub
#then run this which adds new settings to grub.cfg
sudo update-grub

[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Grub2#Configuring_GRUB_2](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Grub2#Configuring_GRUB_2)

> 
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX

    * Entries on this line are added to the end of the 'linux' command line (GRUB legacy's "kernel" line) for both normal and recovery modes. It is used to pass options to the kernel. 

GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="quiet splash"

    * This line imports any entries to the end of the 'linux' line (GRUB legacy's "kernel" line). The entries are appended to the end of the normal mode only.
          o To view a black screen with boot processes displayed in text, remove "quiet splash". To see the grub splash image plus a condensed text output, use "splash". 


---
